:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Prison Population
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population Trends

This section provides a comprehensive look at the dynamics of Minnesota's prison admissions, releases, and population, highlighting key aspects such as admission types, demographic breakdowns, criminal offenses, and sentence lengths.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Number of Admissions ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in admissions from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ### Number of Releases ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in releases from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ### Year-End Population ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in year-end population from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Admission Types

Prison populations are shaped by two primary groups: new court commitments, or individuals newly incarcerated for a criminal offense, and parole returns and revocations, those who return to prison for failing to meet the conditions of their parole.



``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Demographics

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Race and Ethinicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, White, non-Hispanic individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, male individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, individuals aged 25-34 years made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about most common offense.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: :::

Show breakdown of types:
1) New court commitments/1-24.99 years
2) New court commitments/<1 & >= 25 years
3) Parole returns/1-24.99 years
4) Parole returns/<1 & >= 25 years


``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Focusing on New Court Commitments with
Sentences of 1 to 24.99 Years

For a nuanced analysis, this section narrows the focus to individuals serving sentences of 1 to 24.99 years for new court commitments.

In 2020, there were NA people who were in prison and currently eligible for parole. This group made up NA% of the prison population in prison for new court commitments with sentences between 1 to 24.99 years.
